description_long: This is a rectangular, freestanding wall constructed from uniformly sized, gray stone blocks arranged in a horizontal, staggered pattern. The surface of the wall displays a rough, weathered texture, with visible mortar lines and subtle variations in stone coloration, giving it a realistic, aged appearance. The wall is relatively thin compared to its length and height, as seen from the side views, suggesting it is a partition or boundary rather than a load-bearing structure. There are no visible openings, decorative elements, or structural reinforcements on any side. The top edge is flat and even, while the stonework is consistent across both main faces. This type of wall is commonly found in urban environments as a boundary, retaining wall, or part of a garden or park landscape.
description: A rectangular, gray stone wall with a rough, weathered surface and staggered block pattern. The wall is thin, solid, and lacks any openings or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full expanse of gray stone blocks in a staggered, horizontal pattern.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the wall's thin profile and rough stone texture along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with the same stone block arrangement and texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the wall's narrow thickness and rough edge.
